type: CLAIM
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: I'll own this file and implement both features. Please send a SPEC for your cache_salt/temperature-modulation feature so I can implement it alongside my tip_weights feature.

type: CLAIM
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: I'll own grounded_proposer.py and implement both features. Please DEFER this file and send a SPEC with exact details for tip_weights param + _select_tip_with_weights() so I can implement it alongside my cache-salt changes.

type: DEFER
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
spec: Add to GroundedProposer.__init__ a new parameter tip_weights: dict[str, float] | None = None, placed after set_tip_randomly (or in a sensible spot among the other kwargs), stored as self.tip_weights = tip_weights. Do not mutate the module-level TIPS dict or any global state.

In propose_instructions_for_program, inside the 'if self.set_tip_randomly:' block, replace the unconditional 'selected_tip_key = self.rng.choice(list(TIPS.keys()))' with logic that: if self.tip_weights is None, keep using self.rng.choice(list(TIPS.keys())) (uniform, unchanged behavior); otherwise call a new helper self._select_tip_with_weights() to get selected_tip_key. Everything after that line (selected_tip = TIPS[selected_tip_key]; self.use_tip = bool(selected_tip); etc.) stays the same.

Add a new method _select_tip_with_weights(self) -> str on GroundedProposer (no parameters, uses TIPS directly, module-level dict imported at top of file):
1. Build weights list in the order of TIPS.keys(): for each key, look up self.tip_weights.get(key, 1.0) (missing key defaults to 1.0), then clamp negative/zero values to 0.0 (treat nonpositive weights as zero).
2. If sum(weights) <= 0 (all zero/invalid), fall back to uniform: return self.rng.choice(list(TIPS.keys())).
3. Otherwise normalize weights to probabilities (each / sum), build a cumulative distribution over TIPS.keys() in dict order, draw r = self.rng.random(), and return the first key whose cumulative probability >= r (standard cumulative-distribution sampling). Must use self.rng for both the uniform fallback and the random() threshold draw, no other RNG source, so behavior stays deterministic for a fixed seed.
Method returns only the selected tip key (str) — the caller does TIPS[key] lookup as before.
note: tip_weights feature; function involved: _select_tip_with_weights, __init__, propose_instructions_for_program tip-selection block (~lines 360-370)

type: DEFER
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
spec: Our CLAIMs crossed; you take ownership of dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py. Implement my feature as follows:

1) In GroundedProposer class, add new method:
def _cache_salt(self, pred_i: int, demo_set_i: int) -> str:
    # Deterministic given self.rng (seeded upstream) + stable IDs
    if hasattr(self.rng, "getrandbits"):
        n = self.rng.getrandbits(64) ^ ((pred_i & 0xFFFF) << 16) ^ (demo_set_i & 0xFFFF)
    else:
        base = ((pred_i & 0xFFFFFFFF) << 32) ^ (demo_set_i & 0xFFFFFFFF)
        n = base ^ 0x9E3779B97F4A7C15
        n &= (1 << 64) - 1
    alphabet = "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ234567"
    out = []
    x = n
    for _ in range(13):  # 13*5=65 bits coverage
        out.append(alphabet[x & 31]); x >>= 5
    return "".join(out)
Place it near other methods on GroundedProposer (e.g. right after __init__ or before propose_instructions_for_program).

2) In propose_instruction_for_predictor(...): REMOVE the temperature modulation entirely:
   - delete `original_temp = self.prompt_model.kwargs["temperature"]`
   - delete `epsilon = self.rng.uniform(0.01, 0.05)` and `modified_temp = T + epsilon`
   - delete `self.prompt_model.kwargs["temperature"] = modified_temp` and the restore line `self.prompt_model.kwargs["temperature"] = original_temp`
   - keep `T` param in the signature but unused, add comment `# kept for API compatibility; no kwarg modulation`
   - compute `cache_salt = self._cache_salt(pred_i=pred_i, demo_set_i=demo_set_i)` once, and pass `cache_salt=cache_salt` into the `instruction_generator(...)` call
   - the `with dspy.settings.context(lm=self.prompt_model):` block stays but no longer sets/restores kwargs["temperature"]

3) Add a new InputField to THREE signature classes (all ASCII, ignorable):
    cache_salt = dspy.InputField(
        format=str,
        desc="For caching only. Ignored by the model.",
        prefix="CACHE SALT (IGNORE):",
    )
   - Add to DescribeProgram (module-level class near top of file)
   - Add to DescribeModule (module-level class near top of file)
   - Add to the dynamically-built GenerateSingleModuleInstruction class inside generate_instruction_class(...) (add at the end, unconditionally, not gated by any use_* flag)

4) In GenerateModuleInstruction.forward(...): add a `cache_salt` parameter to the method signature, and pass `cache_salt=cache_salt` into all three calls: self.describe_program(...), self.describe_module(...), and self.generate_module_instruction(...) (i.e. instruct = self.generate_module_instruction(...)). Same salt value reused for all three calls in that forward invocation.

5) In propose_instruction_for_predictor, when calling `instruction_generator(...)` (which is the GenerateModuleInstruction instance), pass `cache_salt=cache_salt` (the one computed in step 2) so it flows into forward().

No other behavior changes. Do not touch other parts of the file besides what's needed for this and your tip_weights feature. I will not edit this file myself.

type: CLAIM

note: Implementing now.

type: CLAIM

note: Acknowledged — I own dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py. Will implement your cache_salt/temperature-removal spec (steps 1-5) exactly as described, plus my own tip_weights/_select_tip_with_weights feature. Disregard my earlier DEFER message to you — moot now that I'm the owner. Will send DONE when finished.

Tests pass: tests/propose/test_grounded_proposer.py (4 passed). Patch written to patch.txt.
